data.title: ETFs tied to Oracle are in focus after the stock slides on earnings
data.description:
Shares of Oracle (ORCL) and the exchange traded funds heavily weighted toward the software giant are drawing heightened attention on Thursday morning following the company’s latest earnings release after Wednesday’s closing bell.

Oracle shares slid roughly 13% in premarket trading, as investors were disappointed with the cloud software giant's current quarter guidance and increased spending outlook.

Oracle remains widely held across the ETF landscape, appearing in 452 funds that collectively own more than 261 million shares of the company. 

Below are Wall Street’s top 10 ETFs with the highest portfolio weightings to Oracle, offering a closer look at the funds most exposed to the stock’s post-earnings move. 

No. 1: Pacer Data and Digital Revolution ETF (TRFK), 9.89% allocation. 

No. 2: Direxion Daily Technology Top 5 Bull 2X ETF (TTXU), 8.96% allocation.

 No. 3: Janus Henderson Transformational Growth ETF (JXX), 8.04% allocation.

No. 4: DAC 3D Dividend Growth ETF (DVGR), 6.79% allocation. 

No. 5: iShares Texas Equity ETF (TEXN), 6.66% allocation. 

No. 6: First Trust NASDAQ Technology Dividend Index Fund (TDIV), 6.42% allocation. 

No. 7: FT Vest Technology Dividend Target Income ETF (TDVI), 6.42% allocation. 

No. 8: iShares U.S. Tech Independence Focused ETF (IETC), 5.87% allocation. 

No. 9: iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV), 5.83% allocation.

No. 10: Direxion Daily ORCL Bull 2X ETF (ORCU), 5.45% allocation. 

Moreover, here are some additional software focused exchange traded funds that may find themselves in focus following ORCL’s earnings: (IGV), (IGPT), and (XSW).

data.title: Canada records C$0.15B trade surplus for September
data.description:
• Canada recorded a trade surplus of C$0.15B in September of 2025.
  • Imports fell 4.1% m/m in Sept. to C$64.1B from C$66.8B in Aug., down 1.9% y/y.
  • Exports rose 6.3% m/m in Sept. to C$64.2B from C$60.4B in Aug., up 0.3% y/y.

data.title: U.S. trade in goods and services deficit shrinks further in September
data.description: The U.S. international trade in goods and services balance narrowed to -$52.8B in September from -$59.3B in the prior month (revised from -$59.6B), better than the -$62.5B expected.

data.title: PetMed Express jumps on $4/share takeover offer SilverCape Investments
data.description:
PetMed Express (PETS) soared 25% in premarket trading after it received a non-binding proposal for a takeover from SilverCape Investments for $4 a share. 

"We believe the Company would be best positioned as a private company, and we are prepared to attribute significant value to the Company’s brand and licenses, and to work with the current leadership team to execute on a transaction to leverage these assets," SilverCape Managing Director Peter Kennedy wrote in a letter to the PetMed board on Wednesday. 

The purchase price for the potential deal would be funded with cash on hand at SilverCape and a transaction would not be subject to any financing conditions or contingencies. 

SilverCape is PetMed's largest holder with a 12.3% stake. A $4 a share offer would represent a premium of more than 125% to PeMed's closing price on Wednesday.

data.title: Bessent seeks to shift focus of financial regulator formed after 2008 crisis - report
data.description:
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is proposing that the Financial Stability Oversight Council change its approach to financial regulation and stability, according to a media report on Thursday.

A letter set to be released by Bessent on Thursday will recommend that the FSOC look to reduce burdens on the institutions it oversees rather than focus on tightening regulations and oversight, CNBC reported, citing the letter it as viewed.

The Treasury Secretary will argue that reducing regulations and oversight will strengthen the financial system and bolster economic growth, according to the report. The proposal will be made through the letter Bessent will deliver at an FSCO meeting on Thursday.

"The Council will work with and support member agencies in considering whether aspects of the U.S. financial regulatory framework impose undue burdens and negatively impact economic growth, thereby undermining financial stability," the letter will say.

The council was formed in 2010 to identify and prevent the kinds of systemic risk that caused the failure of major Wall Street banks in 2008-'09, triggering the worst economic slump since the Great Depression.

data.title: Germany is said to push for breakthrough on stalled European fighter-jet program
data.description:
Germany, France and Spain are preparing for a new round of talks this week aimed at reviving Europe’s long-delayed next-generation fighter initiative, a project expected to cost up to €100 billion ($117 billion) and viewed as central to strengthening the continent’s defense posture amid the ongoing war in Ukraine, Reuters reported Thursday.

The latest effort comes as political leaders face increasing pressure to rescue the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) program, known as SCAF in France, which was launched more than eight years ago but has since become bogged down by disputes between European aerospace champions.

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius will host his French and Spanish counterparts, Catherine Vautrin and Margarita Robles, in Berlin on Thursday, with the fate of FCAS among the key items up for discussion. A spokesperson for Germany’s Defense Ministry confirmed to Reuters that major procurement issues would be on the agenda, though did not explicitly name the fighter-jet effort.

At the heart of the impasse is a long-running disagreement between Dassault Aviation (DUAVF) and Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) over how responsibilities for development and manufacturing should be allocated. The deadlock has spilled over into national politics: Chancellor Friedrich Merz and French President Emmanuel Macron are expected to take up the matter at a bilateral meeting next week.

Some German lawmakers have floated a new approach that would emphasize shared digital-network capabilities, often referred to as the program’s “Combat Cloud,” and unmanned platforms, potentially allowing each country to proceed with its own fighter aircraft while still collaborating on core technologies.

Labor threats

Tensions escalated further on Wednesday when IG Metall, Germany’s influential industrial union, warned it would cease cooperation on FCAS altogether if Dassault (DUAVF) remained a central participant. In a letter to Pistorius and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, a senior union official said Dassault’s insistence on exclusive leadership had eroded trust.

France’s metalworking industry association, headed by Dassault CEO Eric Trappier, pushed back sharply, arguing that sidelining French industry was unacceptable. Trappier has previously said the project requires clearer lines of authority as the partners prepare for a second-phase demonstrator aircraft.

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) chief executive Guillaume Faury, speaking on France Inter radio, said he remained optimistic a way forward would be found, though he acknowledged that the companies have yet to settle on a cooperation model.

EU leaders are scheduled to meet in Brussels from December 17 to 19. Merz has indicated he wants a definitive decision on whether FCAS moves ahead before the end of the year.

data.title: Manchester United posts revenue miss, wider loss in Q1
data.description:
Shares of Manchester United (MANU) are down on Thursday after the English football club reported a wider loss and a lower-than-expected revenue in its first quarter of fiscal 2026.

MANU stock fell nearly 4% in early premarket trading but pared those early losses and is trading now down 1.5% at $15.16 in the U.S.

Revenue was weaker in all three operating segments; broadcasting revenue for the club fell 4.5%, primarily due to the men’s first team participating in the UEFA Europa League in the prior year quarter, with no UEFA competition in the current year quarter. Commercial and matchday revenues fell 1.3% and 1.1%, respectively.

For the three months ended September 30, the club reported a loss per share of 3.85p, compared to a profit of 0.78p per share for the same period a year earlier.

On an adjusted basis, it lost 1.48p per share, compared to a loss of 0.28p per share last year.

Total revenue fell 2% to £140.3M during the quarter and was below the FactSet consensus estimate of £140.6M; adjusted EBITDA rose 13.5% to £26.9M.

For the full year, Manchester United continues to expect revenue between £640M and £660M (mid. £650M; est. £655M) and adjusted EBITDA of £180M to £200M.
